back_translation: Now I myself, Paul, plead with you by the gentleness and forbearance of Christ, I who appear humble when in your presence, but bold when distant.
I beg that, when I have come, I may not need to use the boldness with confidence which I intend to use against some who consider that we live according to the flesh.
For though we live in the flesh, we do not wage war according to the flesh.
For the weapons of our warfare are not according to the flesh, but powerful by God to demolish strongholds, that is, to demolish all arguments.
And every thing that exalts itself against the knowing-of-God, and we capture every thought as a captive to be obedient to Christ.
And we are ready to punish every disobedience, when your obedience has been fully accomplished.
You look at things that appear outwardly. If anyone is confident in himself that he belongs to Christ, let him remind himself again that just as he belongs to Christ, so do we as well.
For even though I take pride somewhat excessively in our authority, which the Lord gave to us for building you up, not for tearing you down, I will not be ashamed.
So that I will not appear to be trying to make you afraid by my letters.
For someone says, 'His letters are weighty and forceful, but his physical appearance is weak, and his speech is worthless.'
Let such a person consider that, just as we are in word through letters when absent, so we will be in action when present.

For we do not dare to classify together with or compare ourselves to some who commend themselves; but when they measure themselves by themselves and compare themselves with themselves, they show their lack of understanding.
But we will not take pride beyond limits, but according to the size of the boundary that God has appointed for us, a boundary that reaches even to you.
For we have not extended ourselves beyond limits, as if we did not reach you, for we ourselves were the first to reach you with the gospel of Christ.
We do not take pride beyond limits in the labors of others, but we have hope that as your faith grows, our boundary among you will be magnified greatly into overflow.
So that we may proclaim the gospel in the lands beyond you, not taking pride in things already prepared in the boundary of others.
'But the one who takes pride, let him take pride in the Lord.'
For it is not the one who commends himself that is approved, but the one whom the Lord commends.
